unresolved_task_required_findings.summary: Copilot tool-off still registers repository-managed tools at the create and resume session seam.
unresolved_task_required_findings.reason: This changes the real execution-side capability gate for both create and resume flows, and the safe bounded fix path is not certain enough to classify as an inline minor repair.

unresolved_task_required_findings.summary: LM Studio config-backed defaults can bypass the bounded temperature and maxTokens domains advertised and validated publicly.
unresolved_task_required_findings.reason: The repair spans discovery, validation, config-default resolution, and runtime execution, so it is a mirrored public-contract fix that should be task-owned.

unresolved_task_required_findings.summary: Ordinary conversation writes can persist flow-owned metadata that later drives resume identity and sidebar display state.
unresolved_task_required_findings.reason: This crosses an authority-sensitive persisted-state boundary and is not clearly safe as a small inline cleanup.

unresolved_task_required_findings.summary: Copilot startup seeding can still overwrite fresher runtime artifacts after an earlier existence check.
unresolved_task_required_findings.reason: The startup replay window is lifecycle-sensitive and race-prone, so it is safer to route it through task-up than to assume a low-risk minor fix.

unresolved_task_required_findings.summary: Ignored-path workflow artifacts remain tracked under codeInfoStatus and codeInfoTmp scratch-state families.
unresolved_task_required_findings.reason: The inline path is blocked for the current pass because the selected tracked-artifact hygiene seam overlaps live uncommitted review-loop state in the same codeInfoStatus/codeInfoTmp family, and blocker_scope was treated as global.

rejected_or_non_actionable_findings.summary: The chat-models BDD step wording still overclaims full-response equality after response normalization.
rejected_or_non_actionable_findings.reason: This is an optional proof-wording simplification and is non-blocking for the current story.

task_up_owned_final_revalidation_task_ti: Revalidate review pass 0000056-20260430T002543Z-86b67f53 after review-task repairs
classification_notes: current-plan.json was read before any other flow-state file, and the canonical plan plus codeInfoTmp/reviews/0000056-current-review.json were re-opened from disk before classification.

The current review handoff, findings artifact, saturation artifact, and blind-spot challenge artifact all refer to review pass 0000056-20260430T002543Z-86b67f53 on HEAD 86b67f53f1e6c4500f135c82c12a3817802d2525, so the review outcome was interpretable without incomplete-review blockers.

The prior review-disposition-state.json was not carried forward because it belongs to an older review pass on older HEAD 484b4dd42126f12d3613547e7df61948878c5554 and already contains resolved-minor history from a different review loop.
All 6 endorsed findings from the current findings artifact were classified exactly once; handoff finding_counts matched the artifact counts of 3 must_fix, 2 should_fix, and 1 optional_simplification.

Findings 1 through 4 were classified conservatively as task-required because their execution-seam, mirrored-domain, authority-boundary, or lifecycle-sensitive behavior is not clearly safe for inline minor fixing.

Minor-fix result 0000056/5 reported blocker `Selected finding 5 explicitly covers tracked ignored workflow artifacts under codeInfoStatus/flow-state and codeInfoTmp/reviews, but the worktree already has an uncommitted modification to codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json in that same family. Per the minor-fix safety rules, I cannot safely isolate a tracked-artifact cleanup without risking overlap with live review state.` and blocker_scope `global`, so finding 5 was escalated out of the minor bucket for this pass.
Finding 6 was classified as rejected_or_non_actionable because the artifact itself frames it as optional_simplification and non-blocking proof wording cleanup.
Saturation and blind-spot challenge artifacts both reported no additional actionable findings, so they strengthened confidence in the same six endorsed findings without changing bucket counts.
